preview_text:
The model favours Australia to win at 44%, with Paraguay's chances rated at 26%. That leaves a meaningful gap: the underdog carries less than three-in-five the probability of the favourite in the model's view.

The spread across outcomes reveals a competitive but asymmetric match. The draw sits near one-in-three, suggesting regulation time stalemate is plausible. However, the model sees Australia as notably more likely to prevail than Paraguay, indicating the favourite is positioned to edge a closely-fought contest rather than dominate it.

Model and sportsbook show substantial divergence. The market backs Paraguay at 44%—nearly double the model's 26%—while rating Australia at 27%, less than two-thirds of the model's 43%. The disagreement spans roughly 17 percentage points on each side. Polymarket prices treat all three outcomes as equally probable, offering no signal. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these conflicting views; the gap warrants close attention to new information before match day.
